mission_focus: ToolCache surface area for upgrade planning

surface_map.summary: ToolCache is a 73-line Effect service with a simple getOrCompute/invalidate interface. It has no TTL, uses Date.now() instead of Clock, and has dead storedAt fields. It's imported by 3 files and wired into the DI graph in 2 places. 8 tools currently mark themselves cacheable. No tests exist.

files_importing_toolcache.role: consumer — wrap() and define() use ToolCache.Service and ToolCache.Interface
DI wiring — includes ToolCache.defaultLayer in AppLayer

implementation_details.cache_storage: Ref<Record<CacheKey, CacheEntry>> — entire cache is a single ref with a plain object
implementation_details.inflight_dedup: Ref<Record<CacheKey, Deferred.Deferred<any>>> — parallel call dedup with Deferred.await()
implementation_details.expiration: NONE — storedAt is written but never read (dead code)
implementation_details.cache_policy: infinite growth, no eviction, no TTL, no LRU
implementation_details.invalidate_semantics: Ref.set(cache, {}) — blows away entire cache, not selective by key
implementation_details.inflight_cleanup: computed via Effect.ensuring on compute — removes key from inflight ref on completion/error

notable_debt: storedAt field is dead code — written at line 66, never read
Date.now() instead of Effect.Clock — breaks testability and time-based expiry
No TTL — cache entries live forever until explicit invalidate()
No size limit — memory leak risk for long-running sessions
invalidate() is all-or-nothing — no per-key invalidation
Deferred<any> and result: any erases generic type safety
No access to Effect.Clock means storedAt can't be used for time-based eviction without changing the implementation

getOrCompute_argument_pattern.code:
const key = `${id}:${JSON.stringify(decoded)}`
result = yield* cache.getOrCompute(key, () => execute(decoded as Schema.Schema.Type<Parameters>, ctx)) as any

invalidate_call_pattern.code:
result = yield* execute(decoded as ..., ctx) as any
if (cache) yield* cache.invalidate()
invalidate_call_pattern.trigger: called AFTER every non-cacheable tool execution
invalidate_call_pattern.semantics: full cache flush — all entries cleared
invalidate_call_pattern.issue: a single non-cacheable tool execution invalidates ALL cached results, even from other cacheable tools

cacheable_versus_non_cacheable.cacheable_branch: getOrCompute with key → cache hit/miss/dedup → return cached or compute
cacheable_versus_non_cacheable.non_cacheable_branch: execute directly → invalidate entire cache afterwards
cacheable_versus_non_cacheable.cache_check: cacheability gated by toolInfo.cacheable boolean AND existence of cache service
cacheable_versus_non_cacheable.note: cache is always acquired (yield* ToolCache.Service) — even when no tool is cacheable, the cache service is still loaded and the cache invalidate is called after every non-cacheable execution

cacheable_tools.pattern: cacheable: true is set in the DefWithoutID returned from Tool.define() callback
cacheable_tools.note: All 8 are read-only tools. No write/mutation tools are marked cacheable.

layer_graph.toolcache_layer_dependencies: NONE — ToolCache is a leaf node with no service requirements

resolution_path.tool.ts define(): yield* ToolCache.Service → resolved from the managed runtime context
resolution_path.registry.ts defaultLayer: Layer.provide(ToolCache.defaultLayer) adds it to the DI graph
resolution_path.app-runtime.ts AppLayer: Layer.mergeAll includes ToolCache.defaultLayer, making it available app-wide

in_mcp-websearch.ts.duration_usage: Duration.Input type annotation for timeout parameter (line 75)

execute_result_type.note: ToolCache returns ExecuteResult as the cached value type.

test_coverage.test_coverage_assessment: Zero test coverage for the ToolCache service. No test files exist anywhere in the tool directory.

dirty_file_status.tool.ts: modified — 32 additions, 18 deletions in unstaged diff
dirty_file_status.registry.ts: modified — 6 additions, 2 deletions in unstaged diff
dirty_file_status.app-runtime.ts: modified — 7 additions, 3 deletions in unstaged diff

smoking_guns.detail: CacheEntry.storedAt is written at line 66 but never read. If TTL support is added, this field becomes useful — until then it's a removed feature artifact.
Using Date.now() is the imperative escape hatch. Effect.Clock would enable testable time-based eviction (TTL) and deterministic test behavior. Same pattern exists in truncate.ts line 57.

The cache grows unbounded. Every unique key adds a permanent entry. For tools like glob/grep/read, the number of unique keys = number of unique tool argument combinations ever seen. No eviction, no LRU, no max size.

invalidate() clears the entire cache. A single non-cacheable tool execution (like edit, write) purges ALL cached results, including unrelated cacheable tools. Per-key invalidation or namespace scoping would be more precise.
Zero test files exist for the ToolCache service. The entire tool directory has no test files. Cache behavior (hit/miss/dedup/invalidate) is untested.

smoking_guns.finding: Deferred<any> and result: any erases type safety
smoking_guns.detail: The inflight dedup uses Deferred<any> and cache entries store result: any. This was a deliberate workaround for generic type mismatches with Tool.ExecuteResult, but it bypasses compile-time type checking.

smoking_guns.finding: cache service always loaded even if no tool is cacheable
smoking_guns.detail: yield* ToolCache.Service is called unconditionally in define(). If no tool ever sets cacheable: true (or all are removed), the Layer is still required and invalidate() is still called after every non-cacheable execution.
